qualifying_org_definition: Any qualifying work-related program or nonprofit that can provide signed verification of hours; no MOU or county-pre-registration requirement found in state policy. Minnesota Combined Manual requires verification obtained from 'the employer, from the SNAP E&T provider, or from the work related program.'
verification_instrument: Signed verification from the employer or work-related program (state standard); Hennepin County provides a county-specific 'SNAP Volunteer and Unpaid Work Experience Hours Log' (daily log filled out by recipient listing dates, hours, activity description, and organization name/contact info). State policy does not mandate a single statewide form — verification from the nonprofit suffices.
hours_formula_detail: State policy and Hennepin County form both explicitly state 80 hours per month (or 20 hours per week). Minnesota follows the federal baseline; no workfare or alternative formula identified.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Minnesota source addresses in-state vs. out-of-state nonprofit location. State policy requires verification from 'the employer, the SNAP E&T provider, or the work related program' with no geographic restriction on the certifying entity. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying work-related program may verify hours; absent a state restriction, out-of-state nonprofits should be operationally accepted. The Hennepin County form asks only for organization name and contact info, not registration status or location.
geographic_waivers: Complex/contested as of mid-2026. Minnesota had a near-statewide ABAWD waiver historically. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 4, 2025) eliminated the 'insufficient jobs' waiver authority; USDA terminated waivers in November 2025. Courts challenged the early termination and USDA reinstated 18 states' waivers in February 2026 pending litigation. Minnesota's 36-month clock reset on January 1, 2026 per DCYF, consistent with enforcement being in effect at that date. The existing record reflects 'abawd_enforced_2026: yes.' Waiver litigation status is unresolved — recommend monitoring FNA.usda.gov/snap/abawd/waivers for any Minnesota-specific reinstatement.
submission_deadline: By the 10th of the month following the month of service (Hennepin County); state-level deadline not separately specified — county instructions govern.
notes:
Minnesota calls ABAWDs 'time-limited recipients' (TLRs) and explicitly lists volunteering as a qualifying activity (20 hrs/week or 80 hrs/month) alongside work and training. State policy requires hours verification from 'the employer, the SNAP E&T provider, or the work related program,' which covers a nonprofit signing off on volunteer hours; no mandatory county assignment, Employment Development Plan, or MOU pre-step was found in any source. Confidence is medium rather than high because the DHS Combined Manual dynamic pages were CAPTCHA-blocked and could not be directly verified — Hennepin County references a county-level 'volunteer and unpaid work experience hours log,' suggesting some county variation in the verification instrument used.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial searches conducted across four fresh angles: (1) work registration/Employment Development Plan intake requirements, (2) FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan, (3) approved volunteer site lists, and (4) case manager assignment as a prestep. Key findings: The Minnesota SNAP E&T manual (section SNAP_0503) explicitly states 'Employment and volunteer hours are not SNAP E&T activities and would be reported by the participant to their eligibility worker. Additionally, SNAP E&T is not responsible for tracking participation hours for activities outside of SNAP E&T.' This directly confirms volunteer hours flow through the eligibility worker, not through E&T — and no E&T enrollment is required. Minnesota's E&T is confirmed all-voluntary: the manual states it 'exempts all work registrants, including Time-Limited Recipients (TLRs), from mandatory participation in SNAP E&T,' which forecloses any hidden E&T-assignment prestep. No approved volunteer site list or state/county MOU requirement was found in any source including the FY2026 State Plan (located at dcyf.mn.gov). The verification_instrument field is resolved: state policy accepts verification from 'the employer or work-related program' (i.e., a signed letter/statement from the nonprofit suffices); Hennepin County implements this via a county-specific 'SNAP Volunteer and Unpaid Work Experience Hours Log' that the recipient self-completes with organization contact info — no nonprofit pre-registration implied. The dhs.state.mn.us Combined Manual URLs remain bot-blocked (Radware/perfdrive CAPTCHA) so direct policy text could not be read, but the SNAP E&T manual sections (SNAP_0503, SNAP_0606, SNAP_0612) were surfaced via search snippets with verbatim policy language. The FY2026 State Plan PDF (binary-encoded) confirmed the state plan exists for FY2026 but its interior text was not extractable. Two converging policy sources (SNAP E&T manual + state Combined Manual via search snippets) plus the Hennepin County form confirm self-reported, no-prestep, general-lane status. No adversarial evidence refuting Tier 1 was found. Confidence raised to high: the all-voluntary E&T finding is dispositive for the prestep question, and the verification instrument is now resolved. County variation flag retained because Hennepin County's specific hours-log form may differ from other counties' implementation, though the state-level path (nonprofit letter) is always available.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: Both state policy and the Hennepin County volunteer log form explicitly state 80 hours/month (20 hrs/week) — federal_80, no workfare formula. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: No MN source restricts certifying org by geography; state policy references 'work related program' with no location requirement — coded 'silent'; federal default applies and out-of-state 501(c)(3) should be accepted operationally. (3) Geographic waivers: Contested. MN had near-statewide waiver historically; OBBBA (July 4, 2025) eliminated area-unemployment waiver authority; USDA terminated waivers Nov 2025; courts challenged and USDA reinstated 18 states' waivers Feb 2026 pending litigation. MN's 36-month TLR clock reset Jan 1, 2026 per DCYF, consistent with enforcement in effect. Waiver litigation unresolved — monitor FNA.usda.gov. (4) Submission UX: Hennepin County form instructions and DCYF reporting page confirm four mechanisms: in person (paper_county_office), mail, fax, online (mnbenefits.mn.gov / infokeep.hennepin.us). Cadence is monthly; deadline is 'by the 10th of the following month' per Hennepin County instructions. (5) Form fields: Extracted directly from the Hennepin County PDF (pypdf). Form is self-reported by recipient (no org signature required); fields include recipient name, case number, DOB, address, daily hours log with date/hours/description/org name/org contact (name and phone), total hours, and certification period (month/year). No EIN, no notarization, no org_address field.
